title: Portable Agent Protocol Manifest Schema
description: JSON Schema for validating the YAML front-matter of .agent/agent.md

protocol_version.description: The version of the Portable Agent Protocol schema.

min_runtime_version.description: The minimum agent runtime version required.

name.description: The unique name of this agent workspace.

version.description: The version of the agent workspace configuration.

purpose.description: A clear, concise statement of what this agent is built to achieve. This guides the AI in generating persona and making decisions.

description.description: Optional longer description of the agent's capabilities and background.

language.description: Primary language for the agent's internal reasoning and user interaction (e.g., 'zh-TW', 'en-US').

authorization_level.description: The level of autonomy granted to this agent.

use_case_tags.description: Tags categorizing the agent's domains (e.g., ['code-review', 'python', 'security']). Used to narrow down tool selection and persona generation.

tools.description: List of PAP skill names enabled for this agent. Each must have a corresponding .agent/skills/<tool>.md contract.

schema_evolution.description: Rules governing how the agent can mutate its own configuration (e.g., skills, workflows) to ensure stability in a multi-agent environment.

allow_self_evolution.description: Records whether manifest-driven self evolution is allowed. This declaration does not execute automatic self-mutation.

strict_forward_compatibility.description: If true, skill mutations must be forward-compatible to prevent breaking dependent agents.

mcp_servers.description: Optional: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to mount into this workspace.

protocol.description: Paths defining the structure of the Portable Agent Protocol workspace.

memory.description: Memory architecture configuration, supporting tiered memory for formal execution semantics.

tiers.description: Definition of memory tiers: ephemeral, session, persistent, and shared.

session.description: Memory tied to the current execution session.

shared.description: Memory shared across multiple agents for handoff and broadcast.
